sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.110 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: The of a finite group$G$is the number of non-$p$-soluble factors in a shortest normal series each of whose factors either is$p$-soluble or is a direct product of non-abelian simple groups of order divisible by$p$. For a given prime$p$and a given proper group variety${ V}$, is there a bound for the non-$p$-soluble length of finite groups whose Sylow$p$-subgroups belong to${ V}$? Comment of 2021: th...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
